tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary only describes two-mul chain reassociation (x * C1) * C2, but this tests three consecutive mul nsw operations that can be fully folded into a single multiplication

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i16 @mul_nsw_three_chain_reassoc(i16 %x) {
#0:
  %a = mul nsw i16 %x, 2
  %b = mul nsw i16 %a, 3
  %c = mul nsw i16 %b, 5
  ret i16 %c
}
=>
define i16 @mul_nsw_three_chain_reassoc(i16 %x) {
#0:
  %c = mul nsw i16 %x, 30
  ret i16 %c
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary examples use i8/i16 types; this tests i64 width with large constants near overflow boundaries to verify smul_ov works correctly across different bit widths

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i64 @mul_nsw_i64_boundary_constants(i64 %x) {
#0:
  %a = mul nsw i64 %x, 1000000
  %b = mul nsw i64 %a, 1000000
  ret i64 %b
}
=>
define i64 @mul_nsw_i64_boundary_constants(i64 %x) {
#0:
  %b = mul nsw i64 %x, 1000000000000
  ret i64 %b
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:

  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary doesn't mention multiplicative identity (1) as a special case; this verifies the overflow check correctly handles identity constants that never cause overflow

define i32 @mul_nsw_identity_constant(i32 %x) {
#0:

  %b = mul nsw i32 %a, 7
  ret i32 %b
}
=>
define i32 @mul_nsw_identity_constant(i32 %x) {
#0:

  ret i32 %b
}
